input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm the name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it (includes android, ios stubs, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps need the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling. The easiest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want the build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workload components:

```powershell
# Modify the VS installation to include MAUI + UWP/WinUI
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --passive --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload installs a minimal Android SDK under the .NET Android paths. To discover and manage it:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was placed
dotnet android sdk info

# If you need to set ANDROID_HOME explicitly:
$sdkPath = (dotnet android sdk info | Select-String "SDK Path" |
            ForEach-Object { ($_ -split ':\s+',2)[1].Trim() })
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $sdkPath, "User")
```

**If you prefer a standalone SDK** (more control):

```powershell
# Install Android Studio (includes SDK Manager)
winget install Google.AndroidStudio

# Or install just the command-line tools
$cmdLineToolsUrl = "https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only"
# Download, unzip to C:\Android\cmdline-tools\latest, then:
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "C:\Android"
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "C:\Android", "User")

# Accept licenses and install required packages
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Discover available platform versions dynamically
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install what MAUI needs (pick the latest stable API level)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"
```

## Step 5 — Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images to pick one dynamically
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "system-images.*google_apis.*x86_64"

# Create the AVD
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API_35" `
  -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_API_35
```

## Step 6 — Enable Windows Developer Mode

Required for deploying WinUI/MAUI apps locally:

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## Step 7 — Create and Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Deploy to Android emulator (must be running)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -p:AdbTarget="-e"
```

## Step 8 — Discover Target Framework Dynamically

If you're unsure which TFMs your SDK supports:

```powershell
# List all installed .NET SDKs
dotnet --list-sdks

# Show available MAUI templates and their default TFMs
dotnet new maui --list

# Inspect a project's target frameworks after creation
Select-String -Path ".\HelloMaui.csproj" -Pattern "TargetFrameworks"
```

---

**Key version-discovery tips:**
- `dotnet workload list` → shows installed workload versions
- `sdkmanager --list` → shows available/installed Android SDK components
- `dotnet --list-sdks` → shows all .NET SDK versions
- Adjust `net9.0-*` to `net10.0-*` etc. based on your installed SDK
